payload.text:
Here is your FULL SYSTEM PROMPT for building the

🚀 AI SMART DAILY TASK MANAGEMENT SYSTEM

With Multi-Language Coding + Admin Monitoring + Gmail Login

You can use this prompt for building the system using React + Node.js + MySQL / MongoDB or any full-stack framework.

🎯 COMPLETE SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS PROMPT
🔐 1️⃣ AUTHENTICATION MODULE (Gmail Login Based)
👤 User & 👑 Admin Login Rules

Total Users: 120 Students

Total Admins: 4 Admin Accounts

Login using:

Personal Gmail

Password (hashed)

Role-based authentication:

role = user

role = admin

Users cannot access admin pages.

Admin cannot access student dashboard as user.

📂 2️⃣ FOLDER STRUCTURE (SEPARATE ACCESS CONTROL)
/frontend
   /user
      Dashboard.jsx
      DailyTask.jsx
      CodeEditor.jsx
      Progress.jsx
   /admin
      AdminDashboard.jsx
      MonitorUsers.jsx
      AssignTask.jsx
      Reports.jsx

/backend
   /routes
      auth.js
      user.js
      admin.js
   /controllers
   /models
   /middlewares
      roleAuth.js

🔐 LOGIN PAGE (COMMON FOR USER & ADMIN)
Login Fields:

Gmail

Password

Login Button

Login Logic:
1. User enters Gmail & Password.
2. Validate credentials.
3. Check role from database.
4. If role == user → Redirect to User Dashboard.
5. If role == admin → Redirect to Admin Dashboard.
6. If invalid → Show error.

📚 3️⃣ DAILY TASK SYSTEM (5 Programs Per Day)
RULES:

Every day → 5 coding problems

Deadline: 24 Hours

After 24 hours:

Task auto closes

Next day task unlocks

Students cannot access previous day's problem.

🧠 DAILY TASK AUTO GENERATOR
Algorithm: Daily Task Engine (DTE)
Step 1: Get today’s date.
Step 2: Fetch 5 problems mapped to date.
Step 3: Assign to all 120 students.
Step 4: Set deadline = current_date + 1 day.
Step 5: Lock after deadline.

💻 4️⃣ SAMPLE DAILY TASK SET (5 Programs)
🟢 Problem 1 – Reverse String

Input:
hello
Output:
olleh

Test Cases:

input: world → output: dlrow

input: admin → output: nimda

🟡 Problem 2 – Two Sum

Array = [2,7,11,15]
Target = 9
Output:
[0,1]

Test Cases:

[3,2,4], target 6 → [1,2]

🟢 Problem 3 – Check Prime

Input:
7
Output:
Prime

Test Cases:

10 → Not Prime

🔴 Problem 4 – Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters

Input:
abcabcbb
Output:
3

🟡 Problem 5 – Valid Parentheses

Input:
()[]{}
Output:
Valid

Test Case:
(] → Invalid

🧪 5️⃣ CODE EVALUATION SYSTEM
Algorithm: Code Evaluation Engine (CEE)
1. User selects language (Python / Java / C++).
2. Submit code.
3. Compile if needed.
4. Run test cases.
5. Compare output.
6. If all test cases pass:
      Status = Completed
      Marks = 10
   Else:
      Status = Failed
      Show failed test case.
7. Update progress table.

📊 6️⃣ MARKS SYSTEM

Each day:

5 programs

Each program = 10 marks

Total per day = 50 marks

🎓 7️⃣ STUDENT CATEGORY CLASSIFICATION

Based on weekly performance:

Marks %	Category
80 – 100%	Excellent
50 – 79%	Medium
Below 50%	Low

System auto categorizes students daily.

👑 8️⃣ ADMIN DASHBOARD FEATURES

Admin can:

✔ View all 120 students
✔ View daily completion
✔ View remaining tasks
✔ View marks
✔ View productivity %
✔ Filter students by:

Low

Medium

Excellent
✔ Assign custom tasks
✔ View ranking

📈 Admin Monitoring Algorithm
For each student:
   total_tasks = 5
   completed_tasks = count(status == completed)
   remaining = total - completed
   productivity = (completed / total) * 100
Display in dashboard table.

🔔 9️⃣ POPUP REMINDER SYSTEM
Smart Reminder Engine (SRE)
IF remaining_tasks > 0 AND deadline < 6 hours:
    Show popup: "⚠ Complete your daily task before deadline!"

IF no task attempted today:
    Show popup: "🚀 Start your coding challenge now!"

IF completed all:
    Show popup: "🎉 Great job! All tasks completed."


Popup appears on login and every 2 hours.

⏳ 🔟 DEADLINE CONTROL

Task visible only for current day.

After 24 hours:

Status locked.

Cannot resubmit.

Next day task appears automatically.

🗄 DATABASE STRUCTURE
USERS

user_id

name

email (gmail)

password_hash

role (admin/user)

category (low/medium/excellent)

DAILY_TASKS

task_id

date

deadline

problem_id

marks

CODING_PROBLEMS

problem_id

title

difficulty

description

test_cases

expected_output

CODING_SUBMISSIONS

submission_id

user_id

problem_id

language

status

marks

execution_time

submission_time

👤 USER DASHBOARD UI

User can see:

📅 Today's 5 Tasks
⏳ Deadline Countdown
📊 Progress Bar
🎯 Marks Today
🏆 Category Level
🔔 Reminder Popup

Buttons:

Solve

Submit

View Result

👑 ADMIN DASHBOARD UI

Admin sees:

📊 Total Students: 120
📈 Completed Today
📉 Not Completed
🏆 Excellent Students
⚠ Low Performance Students
📅 Daily Report
📊 Weekly Trend Graph

Table:

| Name | Completed | Remaining | Marks | Category |

🔒 ACCESS CONTROL LOGIC

Middleware:

if role != admin:
   block admin routes

if role != user:
   block user routes


Users cannot access:

/admin/*
Admins cannot access:

/user/*

🌟 USER FRIENDLY FEATURES

✔ Clean UI
✔ Dark / Light Mode
✔ Countdown Timer
✔ Instant Result
✔ Error Highlight
✔ Ranking Board
✔ Motivational Quotes
✔ Daily Score Summary

🔥 COMPLETE FLOW
👤 USER FLOW

Login (Gmail)
→ View Today’s 5 Tasks
→ Solve
→ Submit
→ Pass Test Cases
→ Marks Updated
→ Category Updated
→ Reminder if incomplete

👑 ADMIN FLOW

Login
→ View Student Dashboard
→ Monitor Completion
→ Filter Low / Medium / Excellent
→ View Reports
→ Analyze Productivity
→ Export Report

**Defining the Tech Stack**

I'm solidifying the tech choices now. React, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript are confirmed for the frontend. I'm focusing on crafting the system's architecture, especially for role-based access and the coding platform with its daily task limit. The evaluation engine needs a simulated design as well.


**Detailing the Architecture**

I've laid out the database and folder structure. Role-based authentication will mimic Gmail's login, though it'll be a mockup for now, but secure-looking. I've designed the student dashboard with 5 coding problems daily, and the admin dashboard will monitor 120 students using mock data and filters. A 24-hour deadline and automatic lock will be present, with a simulated code evaluation engine. I'll also add reminders and a clean UI in both dark and light modes.


**Implementing Core Components**

I'm now implementing the essential components. I'm focusing on the `AdminTable.tsx` and `CountdownTimer.tsx`. I've started the services folder with `mockDb.ts` to simulate data persistence, and the optional `geminiService.ts` for potential code explanation. I've chosen the problems and the student data generation is set. I've selected React 18+, Tailwind, Recharts, and Lucide.
I'm integrating role checks in `App.tsx`, timer logic, and submission logic. I've also set up a mock login. I'm building the user and admin dashboards, and figuring out how to simulate "compilation" using predefined inputs/outputs.

entries.description: App metadata and permissions
Primary HTML entry point
Global TypeScript types
Mock data and configuration
Simulated database operations using LocalStorage
Reusable button component
Navigation bar component
Task deadline countdown timer
Simulated code editor with evaluation logic
Main application component with routing and state
React entry point

payload.text: for this project give me the login page for both user and admin login,

**Revising Login Page Design**

I'm focusing now on aligning the existing login page with the detailed specifications in the prompt. I'm carefully evaluating its elements to ensure complete adherence to those requirements. I'm making incremental adjustments to refine the design's alignment with the desired specifications, and exploring alternate methods to better render it.


**Enhancing Login Logic**

I'm now zeroing in on the mock database and login logic. It seems my prior approach already contains a foundation that addresses most aspects of the given prompt. The primary concern is now refining the handling of user roles within the `mockDb.login()` function to ensure proper redirection. I'm exploring enhancements to the UI for better clarity about the secure Gmail login process. I'll test the login with different Gmail accounts to confirm that authentication and role-based redirects function as expected.


**Refining Login Page Design**

I'm now implementing the polished login page, making it visually appealing and clearly highlighting role-based redirection as per the updated specifications. I'm focusing on aesthetic enhancements to the `LoginView` component in `App.tsx` and verifying the accuracy of user retrieval in `mockDb.ts`. I'm incorporating a visual "Sign in with Google" cue, and making incremental modifications. I'm introducing a background pattern and clear visual cues for demo accounts. I'll include improved error messages and a "Secure Login" badge. This means refining App.tsx's LoginView and making any minor tweaks to mockDb.ts, which is already functional. The design will be modified to support both user and admin login.

The implementation already includes a common login page that handles both **Students** and **Admins** via role-based authentication, as specified in your requirements.

I will enhance the **Login Page** UI to specifically highlight the "Gmail Login" aspect and ensure the visual appearance is world-class, matching the "Secure Gmail Login" requirement.

### Specification of Updates:

1.  **Unified Login UI**: A single, sleek portal for both roles. The system automatically detects the role (User/Admin) upon successful validation of the Gmail address.
2.  **Visual Identity**:
    *   **Google-inspired Aesthetic**: Clean white card, subtle shadows, and an "AI" branded logo.
    *   **Secure Badge**: A "Verified SSL Secure" visual indicator to build trust.
    *   **Gmail Integration Look**: The primary button styled to look like a modern OAuth/Gmail login trigger.
3.  **Role Redirection**: 
    *   Emails ending in `admin@gmail.com` or specifically listed in the mock admin list are routed to the **Admin Dashboard**.
    *   Emails listed as students are routed to the **Student Dashboard**.
4.  **Error Handling**: Real-time validation for invalid Gmail formats or unrecognized credentials.

entries.description: Enhance LoginView with a modern Gmail-based UI and role-based redirection logic.

payload.text: and in the dashboard give the modules name for the today program , in the program page the page have chnge to white colour , and give the language header for every language , like java, c++, python, and the admin has to monitor the students and the daily task competion , the seperate the stundents performs the students and moderate students , good student , and the execellent students according to the student performs, the students after completion the task the popup messsage to encourage the students with the popup messsge like good , very good, excellent, fantablouse completion task

**Refining Dashboard Views**

I'm currently focused on the dashboard's task display. The key is now showing module/problem names alongside today's tasks directly within the user's dashboard. This aims for immediate clarity, making it easier to see how problems are prioritized today.


**Implementing UI Changes**

I've transitioned my attention to the front-end implementations. I'm making the code editor have a white background and adding language headers dynamically. I'm also revising the dashboard. I am displaying task names more prominently and setting up the encouraging popup logic in `App.tsx`. I am also working on categorizing and filtering the students in the admin dashboard.

I will now update the application to satisfy your specific requirements. 

### Specification of Updates:

1.  **User Dashboard Enhancements**:
    *   **Module Names**: The 5 daily tasks now clearly display their names (e.g., "Module 1: Reverse String") in the task list.
    *   **Encouragement Engine**: A new post-submission popup system triggers "Good", "Very Good", "Excellent", or "Fantabulous!" based on how many tasks are completed.
2.  **Code Editor Overhaul**:
    *   **White Theme**: The editor background is changed from dark to a clean, professional white.
    *   **Language Headers**: A prominent header now displays the currently selected language (e.g., "Active Environment: Python 3") with a specific icon/color for each.
3.  **Admin Dashboard (Categorized Monitoring)**:
    *   **Visual Separation**: Students are now grouped into "Moderate" (Low), "Good" (Medium), and "Excellent" categories in separate monitoring sections.
    *   **Real-time Metrics**: Completion percentages for each group are displayed in the summary cards.
4.  **Aesthetics**: 
    *   Refined the UI with softer shadows, better typography for "Module" names, and enhanced white-themed editor contrast.

diagnostics.message: Property 'MEDIUM' does not exist on type 'typeof PerformanceCategory'.
Property 'LOW' does not exist on type 'typeof PerformanceCategory'.

payload.text:
**Analyzing Missing Properties**

I'm currently focused on identifying and addressing two missing properties in the `PerformanceCategory` enum within `types.ts`. These omissions are causing errors in `constants.tsx`, and I'm carefully reviewing the enum definition to ensure completeness. Specifically, I'm verifying the definition of `PerformanceCategory` in `types.ts` against its use in `constants.tsx` to identify which properties are missing, and need to be added.

entries.description: Fix PerformanceCategory enum property errors by using existing members: GOOD instead of MEDIUM and MODERATE instead of LOW.

payload.text: the users can access the page for three times in a day to complete the task, the daily task program for this the basic level to level up level program for the student with the program with the different module, in the dash board with the student name with the task modules name and the motivate throught.

**Considering Access Limits**

I'm currently focused on user access limits, specifically a 3-attempts-per-day rule. Thinking about how to integrate difficulty progression, I'm now exploring how that access limit affects level-up programs and dashboard updates. The goal is a smoothly progressive challenge within the constraints.


**Refining UI & Logic**

I've been honing in on the UI, sticking with the white theme and aiming for a high-quality feel. I'm adding a "Motivational Quote" section to the dashboard and showing "Module: [Name]" on task items for clarity. Implementing the 3-access-per-day restriction means tracking `accessCount` and `lastAccessDate` for users, incrementing on dashboard access. I also made some changes to reflect task modules, and how they reflect on the progression. I'm ensuring the Basic, Intermediate and Advanced structure of `Problem` will show progression.

I will update the application to include the access restriction, progressive module difficulty, and motivational features.

### Specification of Updates

1.  **Access Restriction (3 Times/Day)**:
    *   A new tracking mechanism in the `User` profile monitors how many times a student has opened the dashboard today.
    *   If the limit is reached, the system prevents further access to the coding tasks for that day, encouraging focused learning.
2.  **Progressive Difficulty Modules**:
    *   The 5 daily tasks are now tiered:
        *   **Module 1 (Foundational)**: Basic syntax and logic.
        *   **Module 2 (Core Proficiency)**: Data manipulation.
        *   **Module 3 (Strategic Logic)**: Algorithmic thinking.
        *   **Module 4 (System Architecture)**: Complex structures.
        *   **Module 5 (Elite/Level Up)**: Optimization and high-level reasoning.
3.  **Enhanced Dashboard**:
    *   **Motivational Thought**: A dedicated card displaying a rotating daily motivational quote to keep students inspired.
    *   **Module Branding**: Each task is explicitly labeled with its module title and clear "Level Up" status.
4.  **UI/UX**: 
    *   Maintained the clean white theme.
    *   Added a visual "Access Meter" showing remaining daily sessions.

entries.description: Add accessCount and lastAccessDate to User interface for session tracking.
Update problems to reflect progressive module difficulty levels.
Add logic to increment and reset daily access counts for students.
Implement access limits, motivational quotes, and progressive module UI in UserDashboard.

My aim is to add cards for "Programs Completed" and "Programs In Progress," and update task cards with deadlines. **Simulating Email Reminders**

I'm currently focused on simulating email reminders after program completion. My goal is to display these messages as if sent, mimicking the user experience. I'm exploring the best way to integrate this simulated feedback into the user interface for a seamless experience.


**Refining Success Popups**

I'm now refining the success popups after a task is completed. I've realized the existing encouragement popup in `UserDashboard` within `App.tsx` can be enhanced. My plan is to modify the `handleSolveSubmit` logic to include a "Notification Sent" toast alongside the encouragement. I'm also working on making the encouragement popup more prominent with diverse messages and a clear visual indicator for the email notification. The final implementation includes an animated "Success" popup and a smaller toast notification confirming the email sync.

payload.text: give the all type og throughts to the students to motivate

**Expanding Motivational Quotes**

I'm focusing on expanding the motivational quotes within the application. Currently, I'm working with a set of six quotes. The plan is to broaden this selection, ensuring diversity in the motivational messages. I want to cater to different student needs and provide more comprehensive support as they learn to code.


**Categorizing Motivational Quotes**

I've begun expanding and categorizing the motivational quotes in `constants.tsx`. The initial array is too small, so I'm updating it with many more quotes, grouped into categories like Resilience, Continuous Learning, Problem Solving, Future Success, and Daily Discipline. My focus is on enriching the quotes to better support students learning to code. The UI side in `App.tsx` should continue working as is, keeping the data structure as an array for now, for simplicity.


**Refining Quote Categories**

I'm refining the quote categories and the specific quotes within each. Adding these new quotes: "Don't stop when you're tired. Stop when you're done." (Resilience), "Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out." (Discipline), "Computers are incredibly fast..." (Logic), "Every great developer..." (Learning), "The only person..." (Growth), "It's not a bug..." (Persistence/Humor), "Clean code..." (Coding), "The best way..." (Focus), and "Programming isn't..." (Skill). I'll ensure logical groupings.

entries.description: Expand the motivational quotes with a comprehensive list categorized by resilience, logic, discipline, and growth to inspire students.

payload.text: and the student page and admin page the design has unique and attracted design

**Analyzing UI/UX Enhancement**

I'm currently focused on the UI/UX design. I've begun by analyzing the existing student dashboard. It features modular progress tracking and some statistical displays. My aim is to build upon this solid foundation. I will make a decision shortly on the priorities.


**Refining Visual Appeal**

I'm now zeroing in on visual enhancements. The goal is a more vibrant and engaging UI. I'm experimenting with gradients and glassmorphism for a modern aesthetic. I'll include dynamic transitions and micro-interactions for a more fluid experience, alongside optimized spacing and typography. Icons and progress rings are planned, too. For the student dashboard, I'm thinking of a dynamic hero section and better module card hover effects. Gamification elements are also on the agenda, such as progress streaks.


**Developing Dashboard Elements**

I'm now focusing on specific UI element implementation. I'm replacing basic stats with visually engaging cards, incorporating sparklines and unique icons. I'm also styling category sections for clarity and modernizing tables. A "Quick Insights" section with student performance data is also planned. I'm working to create a consistent design language with a custom color palette, soft corners, and a refined shadow system, affecting both dashboards.
